title: lastrea singalanensis
side_effects: Lastrea singalanensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, in individuals with pre-existing digestive problems. Prolonged use of the plant's extracts may also lead to kidney damage and decreased blood pressure in susceptible individuals. In rare cases, improper use of Lastrea singalanensis can trigger allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory problems, in people who are sensitive to certain plant compounds. Additionally, consuming excessive amounts of the plant can cause headaches and dizziness due to its potential interaction with certain medications.
